title: Controllable impedance
abstract: Devices and methods are provided in which the impedance of an electrochemical structure can be controlled by modifying a DC current across the structure. The DC current may be modulated by, for example, modifying the available charge carriers and/or modifying an applied DC potential.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A device for providing controllable electrical impedance, comprising: an electrochemical structure comprising first and second contact elements and an active region comprising first and second charge carrier types, the active region having a first interface with the first contact element and second interface with the second contact element; wherein charge carriers of the first type are suitable to participate in faradaic charge transfer at the first and second interfaces, wherein charge carriers of the second type are not suitable to participate in faradaic charge transfer at the first and second interfaces, and wherein the device further comprises a controller configured to influence a DC current across the active region in order to affect an effective impedance of the electrochemical structure. 2. A device according to claim l, wherein one or both of the contact elements are semiconductors. 3. A device according to claim 2, wherein the first contact element is an n- doped semiconductor and the second contact element is a p-doped semiconductor. 4. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the active region is formed of perovskite. 5. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the effective impedance comprises an effective capacitance. 6. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the effective impedance comprises an effective inductance. 7. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first charge carrier type is electronic. 8. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the second charge carrier type is ionic. 9. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mobility within the active region of the first and second charge carrier types is different. 10. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein one or both of the first and second charge carrier types is photogenerated, and wherein the controller is configured to control an amount of light incident on the active region. 11. A device according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the controller is arranged to control a DC voltage applied to the electrochemical structure. 12. A method of providing a controlled impedance, comprising: providing an electrochemical structure comprising an electrochemical structure comprising first and second contact elements and an active region comprising first and second charge carrier types, the active region having a first interface with the first contact element and second interface with the second contact element; wherein charge carriers of the first type are suitable to participate in faradaic charge transfer at the first and second interfaces, wherein charge carriers of the second type are not suitable to participate in faradaic charge transfer at the first and second interfaces, and the method further comprising influencing a DC current across the active region in order to affect an effective impedance of the electrochemical structure. 13. A method according to claim 12, wherein one or both of the contact elements are semiconductors. 14. A method according to claim 13, wherein the first contact element is an n-doped semiconductor and the second contact element is a p-doped semiconductor. 15. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 14, wherein the active region is formed of perovskite. 16. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 15, wherein the effective impedance comprises an effective capacitance. 17. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 16, wherein the effective impedance comprises an effective inductance. 18. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 17, wherein the first charge carrier type is electronic. 19. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 18, wherein the second charge carrier type is ionic. 20. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 19, wherein the mobility within the active region of the first and second charge carrier types is different. 21. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 20, wherein one or both of the first and second charge carrier types is photogenerated, and the method comprises controlling an amount of light incident on the active region. 22. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 21, comprising controlling a DC voltage applied to the electrochemical structure.
